description: Google Ads API is Google's programmatic interface for managing large or complex Google Ads accounts and campaigns.
auth_notes: Official Google Ads API OAuth documentation describes OAuth 2.0 for API access. The gathered evidence also indicates a developer token requirement for Google Ads API access, but that is an access requirement rather than a separate auth method.
gating_reason: Official onboarding/access documentation indicates developers can apply for and use a developer token through the standard Google Ads API setup flow; no partner-only or contact-sales gate was evidenced in the gathered pages.

api_surface.breadth: The official developer docs cover broad campaign and account management functionality for Google Ads, including managing large or complex Google Ads accounts and campaigns; Google Ads API documentation also references both REST and gRPC/client-library usage.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Google Ads developer page was found in the gathered evidence set that mentions an MCP server.

blocker: Google Ads API access requires a developer token in addition to OAuth 2.0, so toolkit setup needs manual project/API onboarding beyond basic OAuth connection.
rate_limit_notes: Google Ads API documentation includes quota guidance and request limits, but the exact quota/rate-limit page URL was not fully verified in the gathered evidence. Expect developer-token-scoped quotas and API limits documented in Google Ads API best-practices/quota docs.
notable_signals: Official docs are extensive and first-party; the browser run specifically surfaced OAuth overview, access model documentation, and introduction pages describing the API as intended for large or complex account/campaign management.
human_review_reason: The structured fields api_surface.type, mcp_exists, and access_tier/buildability are supported only partially by the gathered evidence because the exact REST/gRPC and quota/access pages were not fully captured in final output, and no explicit official pricing/gating page was read to rule out all ambiguity. Per instruction, uncertain structured fields are flagged for human review rather than researched further.

description: Creator monetization platform for selling memberships, courses, messages, and digital experiences to fans.
auth_notes: Publicly readable first-party evidence gathered only shows a gated official API docs login page at dev-docs.fanbasis.com/login.html; no public page reviewed exposed the developer/API authentication method. 'other' is used as a schema-valid placeholder because the auth method cannot be verified from readable evidence.
gating_reason: The official developer docs page is behind a sign-in screen, and the public site prominently includes an Enterprises page rather than publicly readable API onboarding or self-serve developer access.

api_surface.breadth: No publicly readable API reference or endpoint documentation was available in the gathered first-party evidence; only a gated API docs login page was found.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or MCP documentation was found in the gathered first-party evidence.

blocker: Public developer/API documentation and authentication details were not readable; the only official API-docs evidence found was a gated login page, so a toolkit cannot be reliably built from public evidence today.
rate_limit_notes: No public rate-limit documentation was found in the gathered first-party evidence.
notable_signals: Homepage/footer evidence indicated Help, Support, Privacy Policy, and Terms links, but no public Developer or API link. Browser exploration found an official dev-docs subdomain specifically for API docs, but it required sign-in.
human_review_reason: Structured fields remain uncertain because the official API docs are gated behind login, preventing verification of the actual auth method and whether a REST or GraphQL surface exists beyond the existence of gated API docs.

description: CRM platform with APIs for customer data, automation, analytics, and custom app integrations.
auth_notes: Salesforce REST and GraphQL API docs describe OAuth 2.0 authorization using Connected Apps or External Client Apps, with bearer access tokens used after OAuth.
gating_reason: Salesforce provides a free Developer Edition signup for a non-production org, enabling self-serve API development access.

api_surface.breadth: Salesforce publishes broad API surfaces including REST APIs across platform resources and a GraphQL API endpoint for data queries.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Salesforce officially offers Salesforce Hosted MCP Servers for AI agents.

rate_limit_notes: Salesforce documents API limits and states allocations vary by edition/license, with monitoring available via the Limits REST API; exact quotas depend on org edition.
notable_signals: Official docs show a mature platform API program, GraphQL support, free Developer Edition access, and an official hosted MCP offering for agent integrations.
human_review_reason: Some structured details remain uncertain from the gathered evidence alone: the exact canonical rate-limit page and a directly read official MCP documentation URL were not captured in the final evidence set, so the mcp_exists/buildability and rate-limit characterization rely on partially incomplete source capture.

description: Ecommerce platform whose developer APIs let apps manage store admin data, storefront experiences, and agent commerce workflows.
auth_notes: Official Shopify app authentication docs describe authorization code grant and client credentials grant flows for apps; OAuth-based authorization is explicitly documented on Shopify.dev.
gating_reason: Shopify provides public self-serve developer documentation and app authentication flows on Shopify.dev without partner-sales gating evidenced in the gathered docs.

api_surface.breadth: Broad platform APIs including GraphQL Admin API, Storefront API, and legacy REST Admin API, plus agent/MCP-related developer surfaces documented on Shopify.dev.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Shopify.dev agent documentation references authenticating agents and discusses Shopify AI Toolkit/UCP and MCP servers, indicating an official MCP-related surface exists.

rate_limit_notes: Official API limits docs describe leaky-bucket rate limiting and GraphQL Admin API limits that scale by plan tier; REST Admin API is also mentioned there as legacy.
notable_signals: Shopify.dev explicitly documents agent authentication and MCP-related tooling in addition to mainstream commerce APIs; the limits page also signals a strategic shift toward GraphQL with REST Admin API marked legacy.
human_review_reason: Access tier is inferred from public documentation availability rather than an explicitly read pricing or signup page, and the exact shape of official MCP availability was not fully validated beyond agent-auth documentation references.

description: SEO data platform offering APIs for search, SERP, keyword, backlink, on-page, and related marketing intelligence data.
auth_notes: The official authentication documentation describes HTTP Basic Authentication using a Base64-encoded login:password value in the Authorization header.
gating_reason: The official pricing page offers a self-serve "Try for free" signup option and describes pay-as-you-go pricing with a minimum payment amount of $50.

api_surface.breadth: Public v3 REST API documentation covers multiple product families including SERP, keywords, backlinks, on-page, business data, merchant, social, and appendices/endpoints for account usage.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party MCP server documentation was verified in the gathered evidence.

rate_limit_notes: The official user data appendix indicates rate information is exposed in API responses under rates/limits with day and minute groupings. Specific default limit values were not verified from the gathered pages.
notable_signals: The docs are extensive and productized under a versioned API reference, and the pricing page clearly positions the service as pay-as-you-go with self-serve onboarding.
human_review_reason: The structured field mcp_exists is based on absence of an official MCP page in the gathered evidence rather than an explicit first-party statement, and rate-limit specifics were not verified from a dedicated canonical limits page.

description: Slack provides APIs and tools for building apps that send messages, automate workflows, and integrate with conversations in Slack.
auth_notes: Slack’s developer authentication docs explicitly document OAuth 2.0 for obtaining user, bot, and app tokens, including PKCE support for Sign in with Slack. Although Slack uses bot/user/app tokens after authorization, the first-party auth page gathered here supports OAuth 2.0 as the documented developer auth method.
gating_reason: Slack’s public pricing page shows a Free plan with app integrations available (listed as up to 10 apps), so developer access is self-serve and not sales-gated, even though paid plans expand integration limits.

api_surface.breadth: Broad API surface documented across Slack platform APIs, including the Web API plus platform capabilities for apps, workflows, and messaging integrations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Slack first-party MCP server evidence was confirmed from the gathered official Slack pages, so this was concluded as not evidenced by Slack documentation reviewed in this pass.

rate_limit_notes: Slack documents Web API rate limits by method in tiers (Tier 1 through Tier 4 plus special tiers), with special handling for message posting and Events API deliveries; the rate-limits page also notes changes affecting certain commercially distributed non-Marketplace apps starting May 2025.
notable_signals: Official docs are comprehensive and public; authentication, API methods, and rate-limit guidance are clearly documented, and pricing is self-serve on Slack’s public site.
human_review_reason: The structured field mcp_exists could not be directly supported from the official Slack pages already gathered in this pass; absence of evidence on those pages is not definitive evidence of absence.

description: DealCloud provides a CRM and data platform API for managing site data, structure, users, and real-time events, with SDKs for building on top of the platform.
auth_notes: The API Keys guide says the API capability must be enabled for the site by a DealCloud administrator or Client Success Manager, and users then obtain an API key from their DealCloud profile. The gathered evidence also references a separate authorization-token flow and MCP OAuth docs, but the exact canonical token/OAuth page URLs and wording were not fully verified in the completed extraction, so only api_key is included.
gating_reason: The official API Keys documentation states that the API capability must be enabled for the site by a DealCloud administrator or Client Success Manager, so API access is not self-serve.

api_surface.breadth: Broad documented REST API covering schema/metadata, data operations, user management, backups, publications/real-time events, relationship intelligence, and SDK support.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The docs site includes DealCloud MCP Server documentation and a site banner referencing a client preview starting July 2026.

blocker: Initial toolkit development appears feasible from public docs, but API use requires site-level enablement by a DealCloud administrator or Client Success Manager rather than pure self-serve activation.
rate_limit_notes: The official Rate Limits guide indicates endpoint-specific limits ranging from 5 requests/second to 1000 requests/second and describes X-Rate-Limit-style headers for limit, remaining, and reset values.
notable_signals: Official docs include SDK pages, broad API references, rate-limit documentation, and an MCP server section/banner, which are positive signals for agent/toolkit support.

description: Customer service and CRM platform with APIs for support, help center, chat, voice, and custom data workflows.
auth_notes: Zendesk's developer authentication page documents OAuth 2.0, API tokens, and basic authentication; schema only allows oauth2/api_key/basic/token/other, so API token support is captured as other rather than inferred as token.
gating_reason: Zendesk's pricing page and plan-availability docs indicate API access is included in standard plans and available via self-serve trial/signup.

api_surface.breadth: Broad REST API surface across Support, Help Center, Chat, Talk, Sell, Sunshine/custom objects, and related platform resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Zendesk MCP server was evidenced in the gathered official documentation; browser search within developer docs returned no MCP/Model Context Protocol results, but absence is based on negative search evidence rather than a dedicated Zendesk statement.

rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit documentation states plan-based limits, with standard REST API limits ranging roughly from 200 to 2,500 requests per minute depending on Zendesk Suite plan, with higher-volume options for some enterprise configurations.
notable_signals: Official developer site exposes a large API reference plus SDKs/Postman resources, and plan/rate-limit docs are directly published on first-party pages.
human_review_reason: auth_methods and mcp_exists require judgment under schema constraints: Zendesk documents API tokens, but the allowed enum does not include a precise first-party 'API token' value; additionally, lack of MCP evidence is based on negative search results rather than an explicit official 'no MCP' statement.

description: AI-powered web scraping platform with visual scraping, scheduling, and a REST API for extracting website data.
auth_notes: The official API authentication page documents use of an x-api-token header for API requests; it also mentions an apiTokenName query parameter for specific analytics integrations.
gating_reason: Official pricing page lists a self-serve free plan ($0/month) alongside paid plans, so developers can access the product without sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Documented REST API with base URL https://api.app.mrscraper.com and reference pages for authentication and error handling; docs indicate API-based scraping plus platform features such as visual scraping and scheduling.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official docs include an MCP Server page describing a cloud-hosted MCP endpoint at https://mcp.mrscraper.com/mcp.

rate_limit_notes: Official error-handling docs state that 429 Too Many Requests signals rate limiting and recommend honoring the Retry-After header. Pricing page also exposes plan concurrency limits such as 10, 50, and 100 concurrent requests by tier.
notable_signals: Documentation is public and detailed enough to verify API base URL, auth header, rate-limit handling, pricing tiers, and an official MCP server page.

description: B2B prospecting and enrichment platform with a documented company and people data API.
auth_notes: The official API authentication docs state requests use an API key sent in the x-api-key header.
gating_reason: The public site emphasizes 'Book a Call' and no self-serve signup or public pricing was verified on official pages reviewed, indicating sales-gated access.

api_surface.breadth: Public docs cover REST endpoints for company/person search and enrichment plus account/API key management pages under versioned API documentation.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or Model Context Protocol offering was found on the official site or docs pages reviewed.

blocker: API docs are public, but access appears sales-gated rather than self-serve, so building likely requires manual approval/provisioning of API access.
rate_limit_notes: No explicit public rate-limit details were verified on the official pages reviewed.
notable_signals: Waterfall maintains publicly accessible versioned API documentation and an llms.txt index, which is a positive signal for agent/toolkit integration despite sales-led access.

description: A RESTful API that allows connections to access Notion pages, databases, and users to build interactive experiences.
auth_notes: Notion documents Public connections using OAuth 2.0 and also documents Personal Access Tokens and internal integrations using bearer tokens for API access.
gating_reason: Notion's pricing page shows Public API and Webhooks available across Free, Plus, Business, and Enterprise plans; only some advanced features such as SCIM API and security/compliance connections are Enterprise-gated.

api_surface.breadth: REST JSON API covering blocks, pages, databases/data sources and views, users, comments, search, pagination, and webhooks.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Notion provides first-party documentation for Notion MCP.

rate_limit_notes: Official docs state an average rate limit of 3 requests per second per integration, with some burst allowance; 429 responses return a rate_limited error code, and limits may vary by workspace plan.
notable_signals: Official developer docs are comprehensive and current, covering OAuth, personal access tokens, request limits, webhooks, and an official MCP offering.

description: Stripe provides a developer-focused payments and financial infrastructure API for internet businesses.
auth_notes: Stripe’s API authentication docs state to authenticate requests using your API keys, including secret keys and restricted keys for granular access.
gating_reason: Stripe’s core API docs are publicly available and Stripe accounts can use the API in test mode without sales or partner approval; some separate products like Stripe Signals are private preview, but the main API is self-serve.

api_surface.breadth: Broad REST API covering core payments plus customers, subscriptions, billing, payouts, issuing, treasury, connect, disputes, refunds, and many other financial workflows documented under the API reference.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Stripe publishes an official Model Context Protocol server in its docs for AI-agent access to Stripe resources.

rate_limit_notes: Stripe documents a basic rate limiter of 100 operations per second in live mode and 25 operations per second in sandbox, with some endpoints such as Search allowing higher limits; rate-limited requests return HTTP 429.
notable_signals: Strong agent-builder signals include comprehensive first-party API docs, official SDKs, Stripe CLI for local testing and webhook development, official MCP docs, and a large documented product surface. Stripe Signals exists but is a separate private-preview product.

description: FX forensic audit and transparency platform with APIs for auditing foreign-exchange transactions and retrieving rates.
auth_notes: Official API docs require an Authorization: Bearer <token> header; docs note API keys with prefix like ipx_live_. MCP docs also mention public tools can be used without an API key, while webhook verification uses HMAC-SHA256 signatures.
gating_reason: Official pricing page lists self-serve tiers including a free Basic plan ($0/mo, 5 audits/mo); separate enterprise-style contact-sales language is only for banks and institutions.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API docs show JSON HTTP endpoints including /audit, /rates, and /audit/document, plus an authenticated OpenAPI spec link.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP server is documented at mcp.ipayx.ai with installation via npx @ipayx/mcp-server and tools for FX audits and rates.

rate_limit_notes: Official MCP docs state public tools are rate-limited per IP for users without an API key; no numeric API rate limits were found on the official pages reviewed.
notable_signals: Hint URL https://ipayx.ai/docs was a 404, but live official docs were available at canonical www.ipayx.ai API and MCP pages. Pricing is public and self-serve for basic/professional/unlimited tiers, which lowers initial integration friction.

description: NMI-powered payment gateway and merchant account service for business payment processing.
auth_notes: No first-party developer/API authentication documentation was verified from the gathered evidence. The only directly verified URL from the assignment hint, https://paygent.com/, resolved to a parked Afternic for-sale page rather than an official product or developer site, so the developer auth model is unknown.
gating_reason: The gathered evidence did not reach any official self-serve signup, pricing, or developer access page. The hint domain resolved to a parked domain sales page, so no public self-serve access path could be verified from first-party evidence.

api_surface.breadth: No official public API or developer documentation was verified from the gathered first-party evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP/server documentation was found in the gathered first-party evidence.

blocker: No verified official product, developer, or API documentation was reached; the provided hint URL resolves to a parked domain, so a toolkit cannot be built from public evidence today.
rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit documentation was found in the gathered evidence.
notable_signals: The only directly verified URL from the assignment hint redirected to an Afternic parked-domain sales page, indicating stale, incorrect, or non-official source data for this app.
human_review_reason: Structured fields including auth_methods, access_tier, api_surface.type, and buildability_verdict cannot be strongly supported because the available evidence only shows the hinted domain is parked and no official first-party product, pricing, or developer/API documentation was actually reached.

description: Developer platform with official REST and GraphQL APIs for integrations, data access, and workflow automation.
auth_notes: GitHub's REST API authentication page documents personal access tokens, GitHub App tokens, and the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN for GitHub Actions. The gathered evidence does not support adding OAuth2 separately on this pass.
gating_reason: Core GitHub API access is publicly documented and usable by all GitHub users, while some specific APIs/features are plan-gated (for example, Audit Log API on Enterprise and Scan History API on Team/Enterprise).

api_surface.breadth: GitHub documents both a broad REST API and a GraphQL API; the evidence gathered notes standard REST endpoints plus GraphQL for more targeted single-request data retrieval across GitHub resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: GitHub documents an official GitHub MCP Server and the gathered evidence states it is provided and maintained by GitHub and available to all GitHub users regardless of plan type.

rate_limit_notes: The gathered evidence states unauthenticated requests are limited to 60 requests/hour, authenticated personal requests to 5,000 requests/hour, and GitHub Enterprise Cloud requests to 15,000 requests/hour.
notable_signals: Official docs compare REST and GraphQL side by side; GitHub also documents an official MCP server available to all GitHub users, which is a strong agent-enablement signal. Pricing evidence shows some advanced APIs/features are tier-specific, but not the core API.
human_review_reason: The strongest gathered evidence supports token-based authentication, but the exact canonical MCP documentation URL in the browser output appears inconsistent with the page title/path shown during browsing, so MCP evidence URL canonicality is somewhat uncertain on this pass.

description: AI-powered research search platform with an API and MCP server for searching peer-reviewed scientific papers.
auth_notes: The Search API docs show authentication via an `x-api-key` header on the REST endpoint. The official MCP docs state OAuth is used for most MCP clients.
gating_reason: The official team pricing page lists the Consensus Search API as "Available upon approval" and notes enterprise offers custom pricing and limits.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs show a public Consensus Search API with documented REST endpoints including `/v1/quick_search`, plus official MCP documentation for AI-assistant access.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP documentation exists at docs.consensus.app and provides the MCP server URL `https://mcp.consensus.app/mcp`. MCP docs state OAuth is used for most clients.

blocker: API access is not fully self-serve; the pricing page says the Consensus Search API is available upon approval.
rate_limit_notes: The team pricing page says the Consensus Search API is "rate limited" and the MCP docs mention handling 429 rate-limit errors.
notable_signals: Consensus publishes both API landing and reference docs, exposes a documented MCP server for AI assistants, and prices API usage per request on team plans with custom enterprise limits.

description: Document-ingestion and extraction platform for building agentic workflows over complex documents.
auth_notes: The Quickstart documents authentication via an HTTP Authorization bearer header using a Reducto API key: `Authorization: Bearer $REDUCTO_API_KEY`.
gating_reason: The official pricing page shows paid self-serve plans starting at Standard $50/month and Growth $500/month, plus custom Enterprise pricing.

api_surface.breadth: Public docs describe HTTP endpoints including /upload, /parse, /extract, /split, and /edit, with official Python, Node.js, and Go SDKs.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP server docs exist at Reducto Docs, including local installation via `uvx mcp-server-reducto --login` and hosted MCP configuration.

rate_limit_notes: No explicit public rate-limit details were verified on the pages reviewed.
notable_signals: Official docs include quickstart guidance, named REST endpoints, SDKs for Python/Node/Go, and a first-party MCP server for agent integrations.

description: Google’s NotebookLM is an AI research notebook product, while the official developer surface evidenced here is the Gemini API rather than a NotebookLM-specific API.
auth_notes: The official Gemini API authentication page documents using an API key. No first-party NotebookLM-specific developer auth documentation was verified in the gathered evidence.
gating_reason: The official Gemini API pricing page shows a free tier, indicating self-serve developer access exists for the evidenced API surface.

api_surface.breadth: Official evidence gathered points to the Gemini API docs and pricing pages, but does not verify a NotebookLM-specific public API. The usable evidenced surface is limited to Gemini API developer endpoints rather than NotebookLM product actions.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party evidence of an official NotebookLM MCP server was verified in the gathered pages.

blocker: No NotebookLM-specific public developer API was verified from the gathered first-party evidence; toolkit development would need to use the Gemini API as a workaround instead of direct NotebookLM actions.
rate_limit_notes: The browser-run notes indicate the Gemini API pricing page includes free vs paid tiers and rate limits, but exact numeric limits were not captured in the gathered evidence.
notable_signals: Official Google Cloud and ai.google.dev pages were reachable and support Gemini as the documented developer surface. Attempting to open notebooklm.google.com redirected to Google sign-in, and the gathered run did not verify a NotebookLM-specific API page.
human_review_reason: A structured field remains uncertain: the evidence gathered does not directly verify whether NotebookLM itself has no public API or merely lacks an easily discoverable official API page. Because the assignment is for NotebookLM specifically, api_surface.type and buildability are inferred from adjacent first-party Gemini evidence rather than a NotebookLM developer page.

description: PitchBook offers private capital market data, research, and workflow tools, including a Direct Data API product for programmatic access to PitchBook data.
auth_notes: The public official pages reviewed market a Direct Data API product, but no gathered first-party public developer or auth page explicitly documents the API authentication method. Placeholder classification used because the schema requires at least one auth method; human review is required.
gating_reason: The official PitchBook product pages present Direct Data and the API as sales-led products with request-pricing / contact-sales style access rather than a public self-serve developer signup.

api_surface.breadth: Official public marketing copy for the Direct Data API describes a RESTful JSON API for calling PitchBook data on demand across private-capital datasets such as deals, investors, and funds, but detailed public endpoint-level docs were not verified.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party MCP server or MCP documentation was found in the gathered public PitchBook pages.

blocker: Public official evidence shows an API product exists, but the gathered first-party pages do not provide public developer authentication documentation or self-serve access; access appears sales-gated.
rate_limit_notes: No public first-party rate-limit documentation was verified on the gathered official pages.
notable_signals: PitchBook publicly markets the Direct Data API as part of its product suite and describes it as RESTful and JSON-based, while public access appears tied to a paid add-on / sales process instead of an open developer portal.
human_review_reason: A structured field cannot be cleanly supported from the gathered first-party pages: API authentication method is not explicitly documented on the public evidence read. Because the schema requires at least one auth method, a placeholder classification was used, so this record needs human verification.

description: Higgsfield provides an AI video generation platform with a CLI for prompting, rendering, and managing videos from the command line.
auth_notes: The official CLI page shows a browser-based login flow via `higgsfield auth login`, which implies OAuth-style user authorization; no first-party page gathered explicitly documented API keys, basic auth, or bearer-token developer auth.
gating_reason: The official site exposes a public CLI page and a public pricing page URL, indicating self-serve access rather than partner or admin gating, though the pricing page content did not fully load during browsing.

api_surface.breadth: Evidence gathered confirms a CLI and GitHub repository, but no first-party page retrieved during this pass explicitly documented a public REST or GraphQL API reference. The visible surface is the CLI itself, with mention of MCP on the site but no verified protocol details from the fetched pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The official Higgsfield CLI page visibly referenced an MCP section/tab, indicating some MCP offering exists, but the specific implementation details were not fully extracted in this pass.

blocker: No explicitly documented public REST/GraphQL API surface was verified from the gathered first-party pages; toolkit development would likely need to wrap the CLI and/or confirm MCP details manually.
rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit documentation was found in the gathered official pages.
notable_signals: Official CLI page exists at higgsfield.ai/cli; official GitHub repository exists at github.com/higgsfield-ai/cli; the site appears to reference MCP and pricing, but some pages were JS-rendered or remained on loading skeletons during browsing.
human_review_reason: Structured fields remain uncertain because the gathered first-party evidence did not explicitly document the developer auth mechanism beyond a login command, did not expose a readable pricing page body, and did not verify whether an actual public API exists versus only a CLI/MCP surface.
